pre_conversation.line: Atreus: Father, why do we always have to fight monsters? Can't we just explore and learn more about the world?
Atreus: I know I’m young, but I can help! I’m strong, just like you, and I’m learning to be brave.
Atreus: Look at all these runes, Father! They’re like the ones in the stories. Maybe we can find out what they mean.
Atreus: Sometimes I wonder if we’re doing the right thing. What if there’s another way to solve our problems?
Atreus: I think I’ve figured out how to use this bow better. Let me show you, Father!
Atreus: You always seem so serious. Is there ever a time when you just want to relax and enjoy the moment?
Atreus: I read about a place where the gods and giants lived in peace. Do you think such a place could really exist?
Atreus: The forest is so quiet today. It feels like something big is about to happen.
Atreus: I’ve been thinking about what you told me. About being strong and brave. I want to make you proud.
Atreus: Father, do you ever get tired of fighting? Sometimes I wish we could just find a place to rest.
Atreus: I’m scared, but I know we have to keep moving forward. We have to finish what we started.
Atreus: I’ve been practicing my runes. Maybe I can help us with the magic we need to get through this.
Atreus: Father, remember when you said that even the gods had to face their fears? I think I’m starting to understand what you meant.
Atreus: There’s so much to learn out here. Every day feels like a new adventure. I just hope we can handle whatever comes next.
Atreus: I’ve been thinking a lot about our journey. It feels like we’re part of something much bigger than ourselves.
Atreus: You always say that we’re not alone in this fight. Do you really believe that? I hope we find others who can help us.
Atreus: Father, I want to be strong like you. But I also want to be kind and understand the world around me.
Atreus: Do you think we’ll ever be able to go back to how things were before? I miss the times when it was just us.
Atreus: I’m learning so much from you. I hope I can be as wise and brave as you one day.
Atreus: I feel like we’re on the edge of something great. Let’s make sure we’re ready for whatever comes our way.
